notes.anchor: I was away on my journey to Greece
notes.note: In July 44 BC Cicero set sail for Greece intending to wait out the political crisis near his son, then studying at Athens; contrary winds and news that conditions might improve drove him back to Italy, where he plunged into the campaign against Antony.
Plancus had been designated consul for 42 BC (with Decimus Brutus) under Caesar's forward arrangements; Cicero fears the republic may not survive the eighteen-month wait until Plancus takes office.
Gaius Furnius, Plancus's legate in Gaul and his regular courier to Cicero; recommending him to a governor's favour was a routine and valued service in Roman politics.
